commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch conditionally packages the QA Lab plugin, private QA CLI, and built UI into Docker images and adds environment-overridable platform selection to the production-store package filter.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +137, Other +12. Total +164 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: no. Current main runs the filter inside the target-platform Node image and already configures Linux, the executing target architecture, and glibc, while the PR supplies no before-fix cross-platform failure demonstrating otherwise.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The blocking issue has a narrow mechanical repair: eliminate the unused selector source or connect it consistently to the actual Docker target path.

target.title: fix(docker): make QA Lab image platform-safe
target.body:
## What was broken

`pnpm qa:lab:up` builds a Docker image and starts three services:

- `qa-mock-openai`
- `qa-lab`
- `openclaw-qa-gateway`

The Docker build seeds pnpm's offline store before pruning dev dependencies. That seed list comes from the production dependency graph. Some agent packages publish native packages for several platforms, for example macOS, Windows, and Linux variants.

Before this fix, the Docker store seed could include native packages for the wrong platform. A Linux Docker image could try to add macOS or Windows native packages, which can time out or fail before QA Lab starts.

## How this fixes it

- The store-seed script now filters packages by the target Docker OS, CPU, and libc before calling `pnpm store add`.
- Tests can set the target platform explicitly, so macOS can test Linux Docker filtering.
- The QA Lab Docker harness enables the private QA CLI inside the container.
- The generated QA gateway config includes the `qa-lab` plugin.
- The Dockerfile only builds/copies the QA Lab UI when the `qa-lab` plugin is bundled, and copies the built UI into the runtime image.

## Real Docker proof

Ran on this PR head:

```sh
pnpm qa:lab:up --output-dir .artifacts/qa-docker-pr92087-proof
```

Result:

```text
QA docker dir: .artifacts/qa-docker-pr92087-proof
QA Lab UI: http://127.0.0.1:43124
Gateway UI: http://127.0.0.1:18789/
Stop: docker compose -f .artifacts/qa-docker-pr92087-proof/docker-compose.qa.yml down
```

Docker build proof:

```text
VCS Revision: e0d4ba61d5e4b4ff81b06ea6fe61702868e4eee5
Build Steps: 52/52
Build arg: OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS=qa-channel qa-lab
```

Compose proof:

```text
qa-docker-pr92087-proof-openclaw-qa-gateway-1   Up (healthy)   127.0.0.1:18789->18789/tcp
qa-docker-pr92087-proof-qa-lab-1                Up (healthy)   127.0.0.1:43124->43123/tcp
qa-docker-pr92087-proof-qa-mock-openai-1        Up (healthy)
```

Health checks:

```sh
curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:43124/healthz
curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz
```

Both returned:

```json
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```

Cleanup completed:

```sh
docker compose -f .artifacts/qa-docker-pr92087-proof/docker-compose.qa.yml down --remove-orphans
```

## Focused tests

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/docker-harness.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/qa-gateway-config.test.ts src/dockerfile.test.ts test/scripts/list-prod-store-packages.test.ts
```

Passed: 3 Vitest shards, 46 tests.
target.author: jesse-merhi

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
